title: Lambda execution environments, concurrency, and limits
description: Covers the warm execution environment lifecycle, reserved vs provisioned concurrency, burst limits, and how throttling surfaces as 429/TooManyRequests.
body: Use when reasoning about Lambda scaling behavior. Do not conflate reserved concurrency (a cap and guarantee) with provisioned concurrency (pre-warmed environments). Actions: remember each environment handles one request at a time, account concurrency is shared and bursts then scales by a fixed step, reserved concurrency both guarantees and caps a function, and exceeding it returns Throttles/429. Inspect ConcurrentExecutions and Throttles to confirm. Validation signals include Throttles at zero under load and ConcurrentExecutions tracking the reserved value. Risk boundary: altering account-wide concurrency starves other functions and is human-gated.
